summary: Rare soundboard capture of the Carbondale show offers decent audio but a shaky performance marred by Jerry’s valium episode; the revival of “Don’t Ease Me In” and a solid “Bertha” stand out.
review: The SBD recording is surprisingly clear for a partial tape, preserving a unique Carbondale gig that many fans only know from legend. Musically the night is uneven – Jerry’s heavy valium intake left him drowsy on several songs, yet moments like the tight “Don’t Ease Me In” and an articulate “Jack A Roe” showcase the band’s resilience. The setlist is incomplete, missing the entire second half, which limits a full assessment of the concert flow.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is average to below‑average due to widespread reports of Jerry’s poor condition and missing second half, while recording quality is good (rare soundboard) – the weighted blend yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear SBD capture; rare for the era. Audio is solid but the tape is incomplete, missing the entire second set and several songs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Partial first set is present; the band’s set order appears to be re‑working, but the missing second half makes the overall flow hard to judge.

band_member_comments.Jerry: He took four valiums before the show, sounding drowsy and even nodding off onstage; however one reviewer praised his fast, articulate guitar work and clear vocal on “Don't Ease Me In”.
band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer noted Bob sounded great with solid vocals, despite other comments about later shows where he struggled.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum break was highlighted as a deft segue that recaptured attention.
